segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He�d been three days out of Ashford when he smelled the smoke�the wrong kind, not hearth-fire or forge-smoke but the flat, mineral bite of stone that had changed its mind about being stone. He�d been heading nowhere in particular, the way a man does when the war is over and the shape of peacetime hasn�t settled yet. A hedge-knight�s work: a missing horse here, a disputed boundary there. Small things. Useful things. Things that didn�t ask him to be more than he was.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Then the smoke.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornwick still smoked when Caelin rode in at first light.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Ash muffled the cobbles, the horse�s hooves swallowed by the soft texture and depth of the soot-packed street. Melted stone and hot glass mingling with the acrid bite of charred timber. Houses hadn't burned so much as slumped: wooden beams turned translucent, doorframes sagging into glossy curves, window mullions weeping clear droplets that froze mid-fall. Even the lane stones had softened and set again, their surfaces ridged like cooled lava.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He'd seen dragonfire before�in the war, villages reduced to char and ash in minutes. But this was different. This fire hadn't consumed. It had transformed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A cry�thin, desperate, trapped�cut through the silence.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He ran.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: At the end of the south lane, a cottage had folded in on itself like a paper lantern crushed by an invisible fist.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Hold on!

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Where are you?

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Here,

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: a small voice gasped, barely audible through the rubble.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: It's hot�please�

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He found her beneath a collapsed roof beam: maybe eight years old, freckled face streaked with soot, pinned by timber that still shimmered with residual heat. Her eyes were wide with terror, tracking the glow in the wood above her.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: You're going to be all right,

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, setting his palm against the scorching beam.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He called the flame the way he always had�shaping heat to eat along the grain, to reduce wood to ash and set her free. But the magic answered differently this time. The beam didn't char or crumble. It softened. Fibers gave way beneath his touch, going glassy and viscous, sloughing under his hand in a slow, honey-thick peel. He drew the beam aside in a translucent sheet; the instant it left contact with his skin, it hardened with a sharp crack and shattered.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The girl stared up at him with wonder and fear in equal measure.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He stared at his own hand.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Fire shouldn't do this,

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he murmured, more to himself than to her.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He gathered the child up in his arms, feeling how she trembled against him.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: What's your name?

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Mira.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: I'm Caelin. Close your eyes now. The air's bad out here.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Outside, where the square's wind provided some relief from the smoke, she curled into his travel cloak like it was the only safe thing left in the world. Her lips trembled as she tried to form words.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Mama said to hide,

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: But the red lady saw me anyway. She spoke to me.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: The dragon?

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin asked, though something in his chest already knew the answer.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Mira nodded, her small fingers clutching his cloak tighter.

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: She said she was looking for the ember-born. She said she was sorry but she had to know.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The child's voice dropped to barely audible.

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: Then she sang something. A rhyme. Over and over while the fire came. I... I remember it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Before he could respond, Elder Gareth emerged from the smoke�one cheek blistered raw, his beard singed away on that side, but his eyes still steady with the kind of strength that comes from bearing witness to catastrophe.

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: You saved one,

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: the old man rasped, his voice thick with smoke and grief.

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: The gods bless you for it.

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: She wasn't hunting,

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: She kept saying she was sorry. That she had to find someone.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth nodded grimly.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: Dragons kill clean when they feed�quick, then gone. This was deliberate. Methodical. She melted the shrine down to its foundation stones but left the granary untouched. Destroyed the inn but spared the healer's cottage.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: His eyes met Caelin's.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: She was testing. Calling out in the old tongue, waiting for something to answer.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Something prickled along Caelin's forearms, a readiness sharpening his awareness. He'd spent years studying fragments of Draconic in dusty libraries, and his dreams had carried sounds and syllables that weren't his own. Now the anticipation felt physical, like standing on the edge of a precipice, waiting for the moment gravity takes hold.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Mira tugged at his sleeve with surprising insistence.

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: The rhyme. Do you want to hear it?

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Please,

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, though the answer was already pressing against his ribs like a held breath.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The child swallowed hard and recited in a thin, earnest voice:

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: Nine the arts and nine the lights,

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: Nine the souls to set them right;

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: Keep the chain and guard the frame�

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: Wake not the Tenth that has no name.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The words settled into him like keys finding locks, fitting into spaces he hadn't known were waiting to be filled.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth pointed beyond the mill with a hand that trembled slightly.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: Her trail ends past the hedgerow. The ground there is...

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He struggled for words.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: The earth has turned to glass. Black glass, like obsidian. There's a crater, and at the center...

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He shook his head.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: You should see it yourself.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: I'll go,

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, because any other response felt like turning away from something that had been calling him for years.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He didn�t look back at Mira. He was afraid if he did he would stay, and staying felt like the wrong kind of cowardice.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin left Mira in the elder's care and followed the path of scorched hedge and melted stone. The destruction formed a clear line�deliberate, purposeful�leading away from the village center toward the fields beyond.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Mira�s rhyme clung to him like smoke. Each word seemed to settle behind his ribs, waiting for something that hadn�t yet awoken.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Where the hedgerow ended, the earth had transformed entirely.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A shallow crater yawned before him, perhaps fifty feet across. Its floor and walls were black glass�pure obsidian that caught the morning light and threw it back in dark rainbows. Crystalline spires jutted up from the rim like the fingers of a buried giant reaching for sky. Steam rose in lazy spirals, carrying the sharp mineral scent of transformed stone.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: At the center lay the dragon.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She was vast beyond mortal scale, easily the size of Gareth's barn. Her hide had dulled from coal-red to ash-crimson, fissured along her flanks where internal heat still glowed like forge embers struggling against the dark. Each breath came as a failing rasp, wet and rattling. Her wings lay crumpled and torn, one bent at an angle that made Caelin wince. But her eyes�old gold, ancient as mountains�still held purpose.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin slid down the crater's inner slope. The glass sang beneath his boots, a low crystalline hum that resonated in his chest.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The dragon's eye tracked his descent and fixed on him.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her voice arrived not as sound but as meaning pressing directly into his mind�old Draconic rendered crystalline and clear, as if a fog he hadn't known was there had suddenly lifted.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: At last,

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: The bloodline answers.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He went to one knee, instinct deeper than thought.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Why did you do this? All those people�

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: I had no choice.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: came the answer, heavy with genuine regret.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: I am dying. I felt it three days ago�the rot in my heart, the fire going cold. And I carry something that cannot die with me. Something that must pass to one who can bear it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her eye closed briefly.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: I searched the nearby villages. Called out in the old tongue. Used fire to test who might answer, who might have the gift to work flame differently than others.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: You killed them to test them?

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Horror and anger warred in his chest.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: I tested the fire,

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: Most would have burned. Only one with dragon-blood could have shaped what I wrought. Only the ember-born could have turned transformation back to wood, glass back to timber.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her eye opened and fixed on him again.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: You saved the child. You proved what you are.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The truth of it settled cold in his stomach. She'd been searching for him. And people had died in that search.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: What are you?

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: Vharisax, last guardian of the First Concord. And my time ends.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She shifted slightly, and the movement cost her.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: But the compact must continue. The Nine must be found. The Seal must remain closed.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: The rhyme,

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: The child remembered it. Nine the arts�

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: Nine that weave the world,

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vharisax said. Images flooded his mind�dragons and dwarves forging side by side, a name carved above a gate in a script older than any living tongue, nine pedestals blazing with power, and beneath them something vast and chained, pressing against its bonds. The weight of it left Caelin gasping.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin�s breath had gone shallow. He didn�t know when that had happened.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: The Nine maintain balance,

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: Each school of magic bound into physical form, each requiring a bearer to claim it, to become it. When all nine are claimed and the bearers stand together, the Seal remains closed. But if the Nine are scattered, lost, or broken...

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her breathing hitched.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: The Tenth stirs. And if it wakes fully, the world drowns in entropy made conscious.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: Why me?

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The question came out smaller than he intended.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: You carry the old blood. Faint, yes�diluted through generations�but present. You can bond with what I give you. You can survive it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her foreclaw trembled as she lifted it.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: And you have no choice. I am dying now. If I do not pass this on, it dies with me. The compact breaks. The chain weakens.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nestled against talon and scale lay a plate of obsidian-red, roughly the length of his thumb, hexagonal edges still carrying forge-heat. It pulsed with inner light�ember-motes drifting in its translucent depths.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not a shed scale. An offering.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: This is the key,

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: Take it. Bind yourself. Follow the path I will leave you. Find the First Sanctum and claim what waits there�the Dragon's Ember, first of the Nine, forged for Evocation itself.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her eye held his.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: And then find the others who must bear the rest. You cannot do this alone.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: His hand hovered above the scale, every instinct screaming that this was a threshold he couldn't uncross.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: What will it do to me?

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: It will mark you. Visibly. Permanently.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: No false comfort.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: It will guide you toward what must be found. It will punish deception and reward truth. It will hurt, especially at first.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her tone softened slightly.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: And it will make you exactly what you already are�only more so. The magic you've always carried will awaken fully. You will understand the old tongue. You will see the paths others cannot.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: And if I refuse?

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: Then I die carrying this burden. The compact weakens. The Seal cracks a little more. And in a generation, perhaps two, something that should never breathe air will taste sky.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She held his gaze.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: I do not offer you glory, ember-born. I offer you necessity.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The weight of her certainty pressed against him. The crater, the transformed village, the child's terrified eyes�all of it leading to this moment.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin closed his fingers around the scale.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Heat surged up his arm�hot glass seeking its shape, reality rewriting itself cell by cell. For a moment the world went white, then cleared, he looked down at his right forearm.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A thumb-length, hexagonal plate lay flush with his skin just below the elbow�dark as translucent obsidian, smooth as glass, with ember-motes drifting lazily in its depths like captured stars. The edges were perfect, geometric, inlaid so seamlessly with his flesh that he couldn't tell where the scale ended and his skin began.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: From its six edges, hair-fine black filaments spread like delicate root systems, threading beneath his skin in branching patterns. They ran toward his elbow, toward his wrist, mapping themselves along nerve pathways with dark precision. He could feel them settling, finding their places, becoming part of his body's geography.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He flexed his fingers. The embedded plate flexed with him, the filaments shifting beneath his skin like living tattoo.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: There,

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: The bond is struck. You are marked. You are chosen.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: It will answer you,

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: And it will correct you. When you speak falsehood, it will sting. When you hesitate before necessity, it will push. When you attempt to flee your purpose...

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A thread of dark humor.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: It will drag you back to the path.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: I understand,

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The embedded plate snapped white-hot�a needle of searing light that lanced through every nerve in his arm. He gasped, biting back a cry, and the glow subsided to steady ember.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: Truth,

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: Already you learn. Never lie to the scale, ember-born. It knows you better than you know yourself.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He cradled his arm, feeling the residual heat pulse beneath his skin.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: What do I do now?

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: Follow the flame-path I will leave. It leads to the Emberpeaks, to the First Sanctum. There you will find the Dragon's Ember�the first of the Nine Relics, the anchor of Evocation itself. Claim it. Bond with it fully.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her breathing grew more labored.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: Then seek the others. The bearers are out there, scattered, most unaware of what they're meant to become. You must find them. Gather them. Before the Seal breaks entirely.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: And the Tenth?

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: What's truly buried there?

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: Old,

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: A bargain made in the world's first age. Power that should never have been. The Nine were forged to keep it sleeping.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her voice dropped to bare thread.

segments.speaker: vharisax
segments.text: If you hear it calling�and you will, if you draw too close�do not answer. Do not try to wake it. Not yet. Perhaps not ever.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her chest rose once more, held, and fell for the final time. The glow along her fissured flanks dimmed�and then, instead of darkness, it brightened.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not the orange of living fire. Something older. Deeper. The light moved through her scales the way flame moves through paper held to a candle�catching at the edges first, the great plates along her spine curling inward as they ignited, translucent and amber before dissolving to nothing. The transformation swept forward from her tail to her throat in a slow, inevitable tide. Scale by scale, ridge by ridge, the body that had carried a thousand years of memory began to come apart�not violently, not with heat or sound, but with the quiet authority of something that had decided it was finished.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin couldn�t move. Couldn�t look away.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vharisax burned like a letter burning�edges first, then the body of the thing, each piece glowing brilliant before it released. Her wings went last, the great membranes thinning to lace, to light, to nothing. Where she had lain, the crater floor held only the ghost of her outline in glass.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: And then the embers came for him.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They rose in a slow column�ten thousand motes of ash and amber light, drifting upward as if gravity had briefly lost interest in them. They turned in the air above the crater, unhurried, and Caelin understood with bone-deep certainty that they were not random. They were choosing.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The column tilted. Descended.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They settled on the scale.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: One by one, and then in streams, and then all at once�every mote of what had been Vharisax finding its way to the obsidian plate in his forearm, sinking into the translucent surface and becoming part of the ember-motes already drifting there. The scale flared so bright he had to turn his face away. Heat moved through the filaments like a second bonding�not painful this time, but immense. The weight of it. The age of it. A thousand years of flight and memory and purpose flowing into six inches of hexagonal obsidian set in a man�s arm.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: When the last ember settled, the crater was empty.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale dimmed to its steady coal-glow. The filaments cooled. Caelin stood in the glass-smooth hollow where a dragon had died, and breathed, and tried to understand what he was carrying now.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: From the crater�s rim, the flame-path stretched away toward distant mountains�fine as a thread of silk, bright as a road paved with light. It hadn�t been there a moment ago. Or perhaps it had always been there, and something in him had finally learned to see it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Footsteps crunched on glass behind him.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Well,

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: a voice observed, dry as desert wind.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: That looks profoundly uncomfortable.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin spun, hand instinctively moving to the sword at his hip.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A woman leaned against the glass ridge, arms crossed, assessing him with the calm focus of a predator deciding if he was a threat or opportunity. Twin daggers rested at her hips, their hilts worn smooth with use. Her travel cloak was torn and stained with ash, and one eye was green while the other shone gold, catching the light like a cat's.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her expression suggested she found the entire situation darkly amusing.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Virella Sunshadow,

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Though people who haven't tried to murder me yet generally call me Vex.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: How long have you been there?

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin asked, still catching his breath from the bonding.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Long enough.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She pushed off from the stone and slid down the glass slope with practiced ease, boots finding purchase where he would have slipped.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Long enough to watch a dragon die passing something to a stranger. Long enough to see that very permanent addition to your arm.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her mismatched gaze dropped to the embedded scale, studying it with professional interest.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Interesting accessory. Not exactly subtle.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin instinctively tried to cover it with his other hand, then stopped. What was the point? The scale was flush with his skin, inlaid like jewelry he could never remove.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: You shouldn't be here. This is�

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Dangerous? Obviously.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex stopped a few feet away, close enough to talk comfortably, far enough to react if he proved hostile.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: But I've been tracking something dangerous for months now, so the ambiance feels appropriate.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Tracking what?

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Answers.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She pulled a water-stained journal from inside her cloak and held it up briefly before tucking it away again.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: My mother was a scholar. Obsessed with old alliances, ancient pacts, something she called 'fire-kept covenants.' She died six months ago in the Emberpeaks, and all I have left are her research notes and more questions than sense.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex gestured at the dragon's crystalline corpse.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Her last journal entry mentioned rumors of a dragon searching for something. That was three weeks before she disappeared. So when I heard about a dragon burning villages in a very specific pattern�not raiding, not feeding, but searching�I followed the trail.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her eyes met his.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: And here I find not treasure, but you. With that.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A nod at his arm.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed warm against his skin, but didn't sting. Whatever else Vex might be hiding, she was being honest about her motivations.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Your mother was looking for the same thing the dragon gave me,

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Was she?

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's tone sharpened with interest.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: And what exactly did that dying dragon give you, besides permanent body modification?

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He hesitated. The scale warmed in warning�truth or pain, choose.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: A burden. A purpose. A path to follow.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: How poetically vague.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: But her eyes tracked to something behind him, and her expression shifted.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: What are you staring at?

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin glanced back at the flame-thread stretching toward the mountains, then returned his attention to her.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: You can't see it?

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: See what?

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex followed his gaze, squinting at empty air above the crater's rim.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: All I'm getting is heat shimmer and the worst headache I've had in months. Which, given my lifestyle, is saying something.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: There's a path. Fire, but not fire. Leading into the Emberpeaks.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He flexed his marked arm, feeling the scale pulse in time with the distant thread.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: The dragon said only those marked could see it.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Of course she did.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex rubbed her temples.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Let me guess�this invisible magic road leads straight into the mountains where my mother vanished. Where the last three caravans disappeared without a trace. Where absolutely nothing good has been heard from in half a year.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Probably.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Perfect.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She let her hands drop and fixed him with an evaluating look.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: New arrangement, then. You need someone watching your back while you follow your glowing destiny path. I need someone who can actually see where we're going and potentially lead me to what my mother died seeking.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: We?

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin raised an eyebrow.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: You walk in front,

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: drawing every monster's attention with that beacon in your arm. I follow behind, keeping us both alive with my actually useful skills like not getting eaten. Meanwhile, we both get closer to our answers.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: That's your pitch? I'm bait?

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Mutually beneficial bait.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her mouth quirked.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Look, you're going into those mountains whether I come or not�I can see it in your face. That scale's already made the choice for you. And I'm going regardless of whether you want company, because my mother's last camp was somewhere in those peaks and I'm not leaving until I know what happened to her.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She crossed her arms.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: So we can stumble into whatever's waiting separately and probably both die, or we can watch each other's backs and maybe one of us survives long enough to accomplish something. Your choice, ember-boy.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The nickname irritated him, but the logic was sound. And the scale wasn't stinging�she meant what she said.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: You said your mother called them 'fire-kept covenants,'

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Did her notes mention anything about Nine? Or about something sealed?

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's expression sharpened.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: The Ninefold Concord. She had that phrase written everywhere in her later journals�underlined, circled, with question marks. She thought it was some kind of alliance between dragons and... someone else. Dwarves, maybe. From before the kingdoms.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her mismatched eyes narrowed.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Why?

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Because the dragon spoke of Nine relics. Nine bearers. And something called the Tenth that has to stay sleeping.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The words felt too big, too important for the smoking crater and morning light.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Your mother was looking for the same thing I've just been bound to find.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: For a long moment, Vex was silent, her expression cycling through several emotions before settling on grim determination.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Then I'm definitely coming with you. If the thing that killed my mother is connected to whatever's in your arm, I want to be there when we find it.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: It might not have been a thing that killed her,

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: The mountains are dangerous even without ancient conspiracies.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Maybe.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's hand drifted to one of her daggers, a habitual gesture that spoke of comfort rather than threat.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: But she was too good to die to something ordinary. Too careful. If something in those peaks took her, I need to know what. And I need to know if it's still there.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed steady and warm. No sting. Truth spoken plainly.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: We leave as soon as I can gather supplies from what's left of the village,

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: The path leads east toward the Emberpeaks. The dragon said there's a sanctum there�the First Sanctum, she called it. That's where we're going.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: We.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's mouth twitched into something almost like a smile.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Look at that. Partnership established through mutual desperation and probable doom. My favorite kind.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: I should warn you,

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin added, looking down at the scale embedded in his arm, watching the ember-motes drift in its translucent depths.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: This thing has rules. It punishes lies. Forces truth. I'm not entirely in control of what I might say anymore.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Good,

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Lies get people killed. I'd rather travel with someone who can't lie than someone I have to constantly second-guess.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She glanced at the dragon's body, her expression softening slightly.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Besides, after watching what just happened�whatever that was�I think we're past the point where normal rules apply.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They stood in the crater's heart, the morning sun climbing higher, burning away the last of the smoke. The flame-path stretched toward distant peaks, visible only to Caelin's marked sight. The scale pulsed its steady rhythm against his skin.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: We should go,

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Whatever was drawn by the dragon's fire might still be nearby. And I'd rather not explain to the villagers why I was watching from the shadows while their savior got magically branded.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Fair point.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin took one last look at Vharisax�already transforming into monument, into legend, into the glass-frozen history of this place.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Thank you,

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said quietly, not entirely sure if he meant it for the burden or the purpose or simply for answering the question his entire life had been circling.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The dragon didn't answer. She'd said everything she needed to.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They climbed the crater's slope together, glass singing its low crystalline note beneath their boots. At the rim, Caelin paused, looking toward Thornwick's smoke and then toward the Emberpeaks' distant blue silhouette.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Behind them, the dragon lay in her crater of transformed earth.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Ahead, the flame-path beckoned with patient, inexorable pull.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: And on his arm, the scale kept its steady count of heartbeats, marking time until destiny could no longer be delayed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They returned to Thornwick as the sun climbed toward noon, walking through streets that would never be the same. The transformation was everywhere�glass-smooth walls that had been wood, crystalline puddles where water had boiled and frozen in the same instant, cobblestones fused into organic curves that caught light like gems.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: People stared as they passed. Not at Vex�she drew glances, certainly, with her mismatched eyes and road-worn bearing�but at Caelin. At his arm.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He'd rolled up his sleeve without thinking, too hot from the climb out of the crater. Now the embedded scale was visible to anyone who looked: that thumb-length hex of dark obsidian inlaid flush with his skin, the hair-fine filaments threading away from its edges like black veins beneath the surface. The ember-motes drifted lazily in the scale's depths, visible even in daylight.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: They're afraid,

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: I saved a child,

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, but even to his own ears it sounded hollow.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: You did. And then you went to meet a dragon and came back changed.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She kept her voice low, conversational.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: People fear what they don't understand. And that�

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: a subtle gesture at his arm

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: �they definitely don't understand.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed warm. No sting. She was right, as usual.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They found Elder Gareth in the village square, directing a handful of survivors as they loaded a wagon with salvaged supplies. He looked up as they approached, his gaze going immediately to Caelin's exposed forearm.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The old man's eyes widened, then narrowed with something that might have been recognition.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: So,

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: She marked you.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: You know what this is?

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin asked, surprise and relief mixing in equal measure.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: I'm old enough to remember stories my grandmother told. Stories her grandmother told her.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth stepped closer, studying the scale without touching.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: The dragon-marked. The ember-born. The old songs spoke of them�those who carried fire in their blood and could be chosen as bearers for something greater.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: His voice dropped.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: I didn't think I'd live to see it proven true.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: The dragon�Vharisax�she said I have to find something. Nine somethings. Relics, she called them. And gather others who can bear them.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The words tumbled out faster than intended.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: She said if I don't, something sealed beneath the mountains will wake. Something that shouldn't.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth was quiet for a long moment, his weathered face unreadable. Then he nodded slowly.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: Then you'll go. You don't have a choice, do you?

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin looked down at the scale.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: No. I really don't.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: The mark won't let you,

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: It'll pull you toward what needs doing. Push you when you hesitate. Drag you if you try to run.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He met Caelin's eyes.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: My grandmother said the dragon-marked were blessed and cursed in equal measure. The blessing: purpose absolute. The curse: no peace until that purpose is fulfilled.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: That's about right,

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth turned his attention to her, assessing.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: And you are?

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: Someone following him because he's following something I need to find.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's honesty was almost aggressive.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: My mother died in those mountains looking for whatever he's bound to seek. I'm going to find out why.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The elder nodded as if this made perfect sense.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Then you'll watch his back while he watches the path. Good. He'll need someone practical.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He turned back to Caelin.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: When do you leave?

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Today,

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, though he hadn't consciously decided. The scale warmed in agreement.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: The path leads to the Emberpeaks. The sooner we follow it, the sooner...

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He trailed off, unsure how to finish.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: The sooner you can rest,

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: If you ever can.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He clasped Caelin's shoulder with surprising strength.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: You saved my granddaughter. That's a debt Thornwick won't forget. If you need supplies, take them. If you need silver, we have a little. If you need a blessing�

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: his voice roughened

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: �you have mine. Go with purpose. Return with answers.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: I'll try,

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Don't try. Do.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth's grip tightened.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: The dragon chose you for a reason. Trust that, when everything else fails.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: A small voice called out:

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: Caelin!

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Mira broke away from a group of children and ran toward them, her soot-stained dress flapping. She skidded to a stop in front of him, eyes wide and bright.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: You're leaving,

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: I have to.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Because of what the red lady gave you.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her gaze dropped to his arm, to the scale embedded there. She reached out one small hand, hesitated, then gently touched the obsidian surface with one fingertip.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed beneath her touch�a single bright flare of light that made the ember-motes swirl faster. Mira gasped but didn't pull away.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: It's warm,

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Like a heartbeat.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: It is, in a way,

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Mira pulled her hand back and looked up at him with an intensity that seemed too old for her age.

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: The rhyme. The one the dragon sang. You're going to find what it talks about, aren't you? The Nine?

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: Yes.

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: Will you come back?

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The question hit harder than expected. The scale stayed steady�it would let him answer honestly either way.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: I don't know,

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: The path is long. What I'm looking for is dangerous. But if I can...

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He stopped, tried again.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: I'll try.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale stung�sharp and bright. He winced.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Mira's eyes widened.

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: It hurt you. Why did it hurt you?

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: Because I wasn't being truthful enough.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He knelt so they were eye-level.

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: Let me try again. I don't know if I'll come back. What I'm doing might take years. It might take me to places I can't return from. But�

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: and this he knew was true, felt it bone-deep

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: �if I succeed, if I find what I'm meant to find, then places like Thornwick will be safer. Dragons won't come searching. Villages won't burn. Children won't lose their mothers.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale stayed warm. No sting. Truth accepted.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Mira nodded solemnly, then surprised him by wrapping her arms around his neck in a fierce hug.

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: Then you have to succeed,

segments.speaker: mira
segments.text: You have to.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: I will,

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: When she pulled back, she was crying, but her chin was set with determination.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: I'll remember the rhyme. Every word. And when you come back, I'll say it to you again so you know I kept it safe.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: I'd like that.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth gently called Mira back, and she went reluctantly, looking over her shoulder until she disappeared into the group of children being loaded onto the wagon.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: They're evacuating to Millbrook,

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: The village council there owes us a debt from the grain shortages five years back. We'll rebuild. Someday.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He looked around at the glass-transformed ruins.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: Or we'll build something new. Either way, we'll survive.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: I'm sorry,

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: If I'd come sooner�

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: You came when you could. You saved who you could.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The elder's voice was firm.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: That's all anyone can ask. The rest is on the dragon, and she's answered for it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex touched Caelin's arm�the unmarked one.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: We should go. Afternoon's burning, and I'd rather be in the foothills before dark. Less chance of running into whatever might be drawn by the dragon's death.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She was right. The scale pulsed agreement.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin looked around at Thornwick one last time�at the survivors, the transformed buildings, the smoke still rising from deeper ruins. He'd arrived at dawn to find catastrophe. He was leaving at midday carrying a burden he barely understood.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: But the flame-path stretched before him, bright and insistent and impossible to ignore.

segments.speaker: gareth
segments.text: Take this,

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Not much, but enough to get you through the next few towns. And this�

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He pulled a folded piece of cloth from his pocket, opened it to reveal a simple silver pendant on a leather cord.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: My wife's. She'd want you to have it. For luck.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: I can't�

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: You can, and you will.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Gareth closed Caelin's fingers around it.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Honor her memory by surviving whatever's ahead.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin nodded, unable to speak past the tightness in his throat.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They gathered what little they needed�dried meat, water skins, a spare cloak for Vex�and walked to the eastern edge of the village where the flame-path led. The thread of fire stretched across the fields toward where the land began its climb into the foothills.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Only Caelin could see it, but he felt its pull like a physical thing now, stronger with each passing moment. The scale pulsed in rhythm with it, urging him forward.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: So we just... walk toward the mountains?

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Following something only you can see?

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: That's the plan.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Terrible plan.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: But she adjusted her pack and checked her daggers.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: I've followed worse.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They set out together, leaving Thornwick's smoke behind. The flame-path stretched before them like a thread connecting present to uncertain future. Caelin kept his marked arm exposed�there was no point hiding it, and the scale seemed to pulse brighter when covered, as if objecting to concealment.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: As they walked, the embedded scale kept time with his heartbeat, a steady rhythm that was both foreign and intimate. The filaments beneath his skin had settled into their patterns, no longer feeling like invasion but like something that had always been there, waiting.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The Emberpeaks grew larger on the horizon, their slopes darkening from blue to grey as afternoon light shifted. Somewhere in those mountains waited the First Sanctum. Somewhere ahead lay the Dragon's Ember, first of the Nine Relics.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: And somewhere beyond that�beyond the paths and purposes and ancient pacts�waited the thing that must never wake.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: But that was future's problem. Present's problem was putting one foot in front of the other, following a path of fire only he could see, trusting a scale embedded in his flesh to guide him true.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: So,

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: what happens when we actually reach this sanctum? Do you have a plan?

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Find the relic. Don't die. Probably in that order.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: That's not a plan. That's barely even hope.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale pulsed warm. No sting. It was the truth.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: It's what I have,

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: Then I guess it'll have to be enough.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex squinted toward the mountains.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Though for the record, I still can't see anything except regular mountains and impending doom.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: The doom's definitely there.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: Fantastic.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: But there was something almost like humor in her voice.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: At least we're doomed together. That's practically a friendship.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Despite everything�the weight of the mark, the magnitude of the task, the uncertainty of what lay ahead�Caelin found himself almost smiling.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The flame-path stretched onward. The scale pulsed steady. The mountains waited.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: And somewhere in the distance, something ancient stirred in its sleep, feeling the first link of the chain being forged anew.

narrator.label: Narrator
narrator.style: deep, cinematic, epic fantasy tone, measured pace, rich and resonant

caelin.label: Caelin
caelin.style: earnest, slightly rough, determined, grows more confident over the story

mira.label: Mira
mira.style: small, frightened then brave, thin earnest voice

gareth.label: Elder Gareth
gareth.style: rasping, grief-worn, steady authority, thick with smoke

vharisax.label: Vharisax
vharisax.style: ancient, resonant, telepathic feel, vast and weary, echoing quality

vex.label: Vex (Virella Sunshadow)
vex.style: quick-witted, sardonic, dry humor, sharp and fast delivery
